title: Early days of war against Iran boost satisfaction in Israel
subhead: Israel has continued to bombard Iran with massive, unrelenting waves of military aircraft, while also attacking Hezbollah in Lebanon. Its missile defense system has helped limit the damage on Israeli territory.

title: Israel targets Iran's internal security establishment to facilitate postwar uprising
subhead: Israeli air forces dominate the skies above Tehran, targeting Iran's political institutions and its regime's security establishment in an effort to trigger its collapse.

title: French foreign minister urges de-escalation in call with Iranian counterpart
subhead: Jean-Noël Barrot, speaking on the phone with his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi, called for de-escalation in the Middle East, condemned Iran's strikes and expressed concern for French nationals detained in Iran.

title: Lebanon's displaced have a bitter feeling of déjà vu
subhead: The Lebanese government has reported that more than 58,000 people have fled their homes since the latest war between Israel and Hezbollah broke out.

title: With the Strait of Hormuz blocked, regional trade disruptions could ripple across the globe
subhead: Following the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, maritime traffic in the Persian Gulf has ground to a halt. These disruptions risk causing congestion in Asian ports.

How Iran secretly acquired facial recognition technology through a Russian company
subhead: An investigation coordinated by Forbidden Stories, in partnership with Le Monde, reveals that an Iranian company purchased and then resold the Russian surveillance software FindFace to various entities within the regime.

title: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
subhead: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.

title: In Dubai, French influencers experience new reality under Iranian strikes
subhead: Many French social media personalities have chosen to relocate to the Emirati city, often for tax reasons. With Iranian bombs falling, online influencers like Maeva Ghennam are finding the dream less appealing.
